dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal requires detection of failed breakouts in range-bound markets. Used Donchian channels (dc_upper/dc_lower) to represent range boundaries and dc_middle for reversal confirmation. Entry triggers when price breaks one boundary but fails (reverses back through middle). Exit uses reversal through middle line. Direction is context-dependent: go LONG after failed breakout from upper boundary, SHORT after failed breakout from lower boundary. Stop loss set conservatively at 2% due to range-bound nature of strategy.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal requires detection of failed breakouts in range-bound markets. Used Donchian channels (dc_upper/dc_lower) to represent range boundaries and dc_middle for reversal confirmation. Entry triggers when price breaks one boundary but fails (reverses back through middle). Exit uses reversal through middle line. Direction is context-dependent: go LONG after failed breakout from upper boundary, SHORT after failed breakout from lower boundary. Stop loss set conservatively at 2% due to range-bound nature of strategy.

source_rule_text: When a failed breakout occurs in one direction in a range-bound market, trade in the opposite direction.
